FX: 1 USD = 9.71 SEK today. US prices/values are SEK-normalised; limit_price_sek should be the SEK-equivalent.

JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Portfolio at +0.3%, severely behind floor pace (-9.3pp on day 35). The weekly rotation (ATRLJ + HM-B exit, VIMIAN + LIFCO entry) has deployed most idle cash; now at 31.9% cash with 8 holdings spanning five sectors. The critical earnings cluster lands in 27–33 days: VOLV/ESSITY (Jul 24), HEXA (Jul 25), LIFCO (Jul 24), VIMIAN (Jul 29), STORY-B (Jul 29). No further cash deployment until ATRLJ's Q2 (Jul 10, already exited) and the first print lands. The book is now fully positioned to adjudicate on thesis quality; execution is clean, conviction is real, margin is thin.

## 2. Holdings

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Consumer Staples | 6.7%
- **Thesis:** 512 MSEK insider cluster (7 buyers, 0 sellers, Industrivärden lead) into a defensive compounder at ~14x P/E, 3.4% yield. Q2 Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 reveals accelerating revenue decline.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Industrials | 3.9%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK near 52w highs endorsing forward earnings on net cash, 4% yield, 9.8% op margin. Q2 Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Insider becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order-intake collapse.
- **Status:** `intact`

**RUSTA.ST** — Consumer Discretionary | 3.8%
- **Thesis:** Aforber Invest bought ~52 MSEK across 13 tranches into -23% drawdown post-positive Q4 and dividend raise. Discount retail counter-cyclical tailwind. Starter pending D/E leverage validation (likely IFRS-16 operating lease).
- **Sell if:** Genuine financial leverage >243% unconfirmed, same-store sales deteriorate, or Aforber becomes net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

**STORY-B.ST** — Consumer Discretionary | 15.0%
- **Thesis:** 45 MSEK insider cluster (founder + CFO) into post-downgrade dip. Jul 29 catalyst; high conviction on timing and insider quality.
- **Sell if:** Q2 reveals margin compression or revenue miss; insider becomes net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — largest conviction position

**SCST.ST** — Consumer Staples | 6.4%
- **Thesis:** Broad 9-buyer insider cluster (0 sellers, CEO + strategic owner) on a staples name. Starter ahead of Q2 earnings.
- **Sell if:** Q2 shows material margin or revenue deterioration; insiders turn net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — newest addition, unproven

**HEXA-B.ST** — Industrials | 6.1%
- **Thesis:** Unanimous 4-buyer cluster (CFO included) into 21% below 200MA washout. Jul 25 catalyst; high vol (82%) provides asymmetry. Data quality flag on fundamentals (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%) not yet resolved.
- **Sell if:** Q2 reveals going-concern deterioration; data cleans up showing the valuation was an artifact but multiples are actually stretched.
- **Status:** `intact` — data risk, Jul 25 print resolves

**VIMIAN.ST** — Health Care | 13.6%
- **Thesis:** 128 MSEK insider cluster led by Fidelio anchor-holder doubling up; reclaiming 200MA. Leveraged compounder with double-digit growth (R12 +11.7%) but deeply negative FCF (-243 MEUR) and 2.8bn EUR net debt. Q2 Jul 29 is binary catalyst; thesis lives on insider + momentum, dies on earnings miss or covenant risk revealed.
- **Sell if:** Q2 shows FCF deterioration (worse than -243 MEUR implied), net debt up, or insiders turn net seller.
- **Status:** `intact` — high conviction on insider signal, real balance-sheet risk

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Industrials | 12.6%
- **Thesis:** Clean insider cluster (11.9 MSEK net buy, 3 buyers incl. repeat board member) on quality 18% op-margin compounder. Jul 24 catalyst. Valuation is rich (P/E 38.8) but insider buying + momentum inflection + earnings season justify the starter.
- **Sell if:** Q2 misses or guidance disappoints; momentum rolls back below 200MA; insider selling accelerates.
- **Status:** `intact` — modest upside skew but real signal

## 3. Watchlist

**ASSA-B.ST** — Quality large-cap compounder; CEO + management bought ~30 MSEK (Apr/Jun), but revenue contracting (-1.8% YoY) and P/E 24x offers no safety margin. Entry target ≤310 SEK post-earnings (Jul 28) or on a deeper dip. Jul 24 VOLV/ESSITY earnings will set tone for industrials/defensives.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Herenco bought ~25 MSEK (3 tranches, Jun) into -24% drawdown; small-cap (16M turno) with soft fundamentals (revenue -3.2% YoY). Strong insider signal but no catalyst until Aug 5 earnings; skip for now due to low liquidity and late print. Revisit post-Aug earnings if position works.

**VIMFAC** — Parent of Vimian; holding company structure. Lower leverage, but watch whether Vimian's Q2 implies a parent-level dividend cut or capital restructuring.

## 4. Lessons / open questions

- **Rotation execution clean.** ATRLJ sold at 26.20 (limit 26.00, filled better); HM-B at 166.05 (limit 165.00, filled better). Market offered discounts on both exits — correct to be patient on limits.
- **Cash burn rapid into earnings cluster.** Deployed ~33k SEK this week; now 31.9% cash. Next cycle should evaluate whether to hold that ~40k or deploy further, contingent on how the first July prints land (VOLV/ESSITY, HEXA, LIFCO Jul 24–25). No action until we see data.
- **Consumer Discretionary concentration:** STORY-B (15%) + RUSTA (3.8%) + previous exits of Thule/HM mean sector is now smaller but STORY-B is outsized. Is this conviction or concentration risk? Answer: conviction on STORY-B insider cluster and Jul 29 catalyst justifies the size; RUSTA is starter. If STORY-B thesis breaks, rotate immediately.
- **Earnings cluster sequencing:** VOLV/ESSITY/LIFCO/HEXA all land Jul 24–25. If the first two disappoint, Industrials and Staples risk repricing downward. Monitor correlation — may need to trim or pivot if that sector reprices hard.
- **Open:** SCST.ST and HEXA-B.ST are both 1-day-old entries with real data risk (HEXA fundamentals possibly an artifact, SCST unproven). Both depend entirely on Q2 earnings to validate thesis. Assess confidence vs. position size — neither is oversized, both are defensible on insider signal alone, but the data gaps are material. Jul 25–29 prints will resolve.

ANALYST NOTES on today's candidates:
--- Analyst note on STORY-B.ST (Storytel) ---
# Storytel (STORY-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis:** A textbook founder-conviction insider cluster — Wingefors AB bought ~45M SEK over four consecutive days (June 22–25), with the CFO adding alongside. This is the strongest insider signal type on the Swedish market: a large, repeated, founder-level buy on weakness, with a near-term earnings catalyst (~July 29). At 12.9x P/E, net cash, 27% ROE and 6.7% FCF yield, the fundamentals comfortably back the conviction. We already hold at 91.00 with price flat at 91.60 — this confirms rather than starts the thesis.

**Positives:**
- Insider cluster is exceptional: 46.5M SEK net buy, 3 distinct buyers, zero sellers in 90d; Wingefors stacking on consecutive days signals genuine conviction, not noise.
- Clean fundamentals: net cash balance sheet, 11.3% operating margin, 27% ROE, P/E 12.9 — cheap for a profitable, growing platform.
- Earnings catalyst ~30 days out (~July 29) gives the insider signal a near-term test point.
- 3m momentum +18% holds despite the recent 1m pullback (-8.3%); buyers stepping into weakness.

**Concerns:**
- Pareto downgrade to Hold (June 22) flags rising Spotify competition in audiobook streaming — a real structural threat, not boilerplate.
- News references an "ongoing buyout of the company" — a pending take-private materially changes the setup; insider buys may relate to deal mechanics, capping upside near a bid price. I cannot quantify the deal terms from this data.
- Court loss to Unionen (M4) is a modest overhang, though not thesis-breaking.
- Vol30d at 52.8% is high — wide swings to stomach.

**Insider / news read:** This is the single most actionable item in the file. A founder buying 45M SEK in four days, with the CFO joining and no sellers, is as strong as Swedish insider signals get. News is split: bullish insider/AI-product items vs. bearish Pareto-competition and union-loss items. The possible buyout reference is the swing factor — it could cap upside.

**Price read:** Px 91.60 sits +7.7% above 200ma, -13.7% off the 52w high, basically flat to our 91.00 cost. Buyers absorbing the recent dip near our entry — comfortable hold zone with room to add.

**Verdict:** Insider conviction strongly intact, fundamentals cheap, catalyst near — `HELD: thesis intact`.

```json
{
  "ticker": "STORY-B.ST",
  "name": "Storytel",
  "verdict": "HELD: thesis intact",
  "conviction": 4,
  "key_positive": "Founder Wingefors plus CFO bought 46.5M SEK across four consecutive days with zero sellers — strongest-tier Swedish insider signal — into cheap fundamentals (12.9x P/E, net cash, 27% ROE).",
  "key_concern": "References to an ongoing buyout and a Pareto downgrade on Spotify competition could cap upside; deal terms unknown from available data.",
  "would_buy_at": 92.0,
  "would_sell_at": 120.0,
  "notes_for_trader": "Already held at 91.00, basically flat. Insider cluster confirms — can add on this conviction up to caps. Watch ~Jul 29 earnings and clarify the buyout reference if possible; a confirmed take-private bid would change the sell-trigger to bid-price logic."
}
```

--- Analyst note on SCST.ST (Scandi Standard) ---
# Scandi Standard (SCST.ST) — HELD review

**Thesis.** Held at 144.40, now 145.40 (~flat). The story remains the insider signal: a broad, sustained cluster of buying — 9 distinct buyers, 19.4M SEK net over 90d, zero sellers — led by major shareholder Grupo Lusiaves and senior management (CEO-level names Tunestål, Strömmen). This is the strongest possible insider configuration on a Swedish small/mid cap: it's not one program, it's the controlling owner *and* management accumulating in size. Breakout above 200MA (+22%) with earnings ~29 days out gives a near-term catalyst.

**Positives**
- Exceptional insider breadth: 9 buyers, no sellers, ~19M SEK, including the controlling Portuguese owner adding repeatedly.
- Revenue +8.7% YoY, ROE 14.1% — operationally fine for a staples poultry name.
- Technically constructive: above 200MA, +50% over 12m, yet only flat 1m (consolidating, not extended near-term).
- Earnings catalyst in ~4 weeks could confirm the insider conviction.

**Concerns**
- Thin margins (operating 4.5%, net 2.8%) and high leverage (net debt/equity 71.5%) — commodity poultry economics, vulnerable to feed costs.
- P/E 23.7 is full for a low-margin staple; FCF yield only 1.5%.
- Lusiaves buying may partly reflect a creep toward control/eventual offer rather than pure undervaluation — could cap as easily as fuel upside.
- Still -11.6% off 52w high; the breakout hasn't proven itself yet.

**Insider / news read.** This is the whole case and it's a strong one. Three M3+ news items in a week all confirm the major Portuguese shareholder is buying aggressively. Controlling-owner accumulation + management buying is a high-quality bullish signal on a Swedish small cap.

**Price read.** At 145.40 vs 144.40 cost, essentially breakeven. Above 200MA, consolidating after a strong 12m — a healthy base ahead of earnings, not an extended chase.

**Verdict.** Insider signal intact and intensifying, near-term catalyst pending, thesis fully alive — HELD: thesis intact.

```json

  "ticker": "SCST.ST",

  "verdict": "HELD: thesis intact",
  "conviction": 4,
  "key_positive": "Controlling owner + 9 insiders bought ~19M SEK in 90d with zero sellers — best-in-class Swedish insider cluster, with earnings ~4 weeks out.",
  "key_concern": "Thin margins and 71.5% net debt/equity make the business fragile, and full P/E 23.7 leaves little valuation cushion if the breakout fails.",
  "would_buy_at": 145.0,
  "would_sell_at": 175.0,
  "notes_for_trader": "Add candidate on the insider strength, but check sector cap (Consumer Staples) before sizing up. Earnings ~2026-07-28 is the catalyst; an add ahead of it is defensible. Sell trigger: insider buying stops + a weak Q2 print."
}
```

--- Analyst note on KJELL.ST (Kjell Group) ---
# Kjell Group (KJELL.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A genuinely interesting insider-cluster setup: CEO, CFO, board/management AND the largest shareholder (Cervantes/Spotify-founder-linked Nordkronen) all buying ~7.3M SEK fresh on the lows, into earnings (~25 days out). Stock has already run +47% over 3m and sits near its 52w high. The signal is real, but it's a deteriorating-fundamentals turnaround bet — insiders may know the trough is in, but the financials are still ugly. Catalyst-driven, medium horizon.

**Positives**
- Exceptionally clean insider tape: 5 distinct buyers, zero sellers, including CEO (twice), CFO, and the largest shareholder adding — strongest possible Swedish insider signal.
- Multiple independent holders (Cervantes/Nordkronen) buying same week corroborates the cluster — not one person's diversification.
- Q2 earnings ~24 Jul is a near-term catalyst; insiders accumulating *into* the print is notable timing.
- Momentum already confirming (+47% 3m, above 200ma) — the market is starting to agree.

**Concerns**
- Fundamentals are bad: revenue -10% YoY, operating margin -16.7%, ROE -42%, FCF negative, net debt 463M (48% of equity). This is a real turnaround, not a quality compounder.
- Already +47% in 3m and only -5% from the 52w high — much of the "insiders see a bottom" move may be priced; chasing risk.
- vol30d 50% — wide swings; sizing must respect that.
- Low turnover (2.3M/day) — thin liquidity for a meaningful position.

**Insider / news read.** Best-in-class insider cluster: CEO/CFO/largest-shareholder all buying 7.3M SEK in one week, news confirms (Spotify-founder-linked multi-million purchase). This is exactly the signal the mandate weights highly on Swedish small-cap.

**Price read.** At 12.20, +47% 3m, -5% from 52w high, +26.7% vs 200ma — momentum is intact but the easy part may be done. No valuation anchor (no earnings), so this is a signal/catalyst trade, not a value entry.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is too strong to pass, but the run-up and broken fundamentals cap conviction — BUY — starter ahead of the 24 Jul print.

```json
{
  "ticker": "KJELL.ST",

  "verdict": "BUY — starter",
  "conviction": 3,

  "key_concern": "Fundamentals are deeply negative (op margin -16.7%, ROE -42%, neg FCF) and the stock is already +47% in 3m near its 52w high.",
  "would_buy_at": 12.50,
  "would_sell_at": 16.00,

}
```

--- Analyst note on NOLA-B.ST (Nolato) ---
# Nolato (NOLA-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A large, repeated insider cluster from anchor shareholder Herenco (~25M SEK across three June buys) into a stock sitting -12% below its 200ma and -24% off the 52w high. The signal is genuine conviction from someone who knows the business, into weakness. Upside case is mean-reversion toward the 200ma plus an improving operational story (Romania divestiture = portfolio cleanup). Modest 6–12mo compounder candidate, not a near-term catalyst trade.

**Positives**
- Herenco (the controlling family holding) added ~25M SEK across three separate buys in June — concentrated, conviction-driven accumulation into weakness, the strongest insider pattern we look for.
- Stock is oversold technically: -12% vs 200ma, -24% off 52w high, with stable 3m return — base may be forming.
- Decent quality: ROE 13%, 10.8% operating margin, FCF positive (442 MSEK), 3.5% dividend yield, manageable net debt (0.2x equity).
- Romania divestiture suggests active portfolio pruning, potentially margin-accretive.

**Concerns**
- Revenue is shrinking: -3.2% YoY, -4.2% 3y CAGR. This is a melting-ice-cube top line, not a growth story — the insider buy is the only fresh catalyst.
- P/E 17.2 isn't cheap for a no-growth, mid-margin materials/contract-manufacturing name; the value here is the signal, not the multiple.
- Next earnings ~Aug 5 (~37 days out) — a near-term operational disappointment could overwhelm the technical setup.
- Volatility moderate (27%); turnover decent (16M) so liquidity is fine, but no momentum confirmation yet (-5.6% 1m).

**Insider / news read.** Strong Swedish insider signal — Herenco buying ~25M SEK in three tranches in two weeks, plus a smaller board-member buy. This is the controlling holding adding to an already large stake, which is meaningful conviction rather than diversification noise. News flow is dominated by these buys plus the Romania divestiture (housekeeping).

**Price read.** At 48.45, the name is well below trend (200ma -12%, 52wH -24%) and basing rather than breaking down (3m +3.3%). The insider cluster gives a reason the bottom may be in, but there's no momentum confirmation yet.

**Verdict.** Strong insider signal into an oversold, decent-quality name, but a declining top line and full multiple cap the upside — BUY — starter.

```json
{
```

--- Analyst note on CAT (Caterpillar) ---
**Thesis** — CAT has gone parabolic: +170% over 12m, +48.6% in 3m, sitting at a 52w high on a +50% extension above its 200MA. That's a powerful momentum move but the fundamentals don't justify the re-rating — revenue is growing ~12% YoY and EPS is solid, but a P/E of 51 on a cyclical heavy-equipment maker is extreme. This is a late-stage momentum chase, not a value setup.

**Positives**
- Strong momentum and clean breakout to new highs with high liquidity (3.2B turnover).
- Genuinely good operating business: ROE 50.5%, 16.5% operating margin, $8.8B FCF.
- Just raised dividend 8% — signals management confidence.

**Concerns**
- P/E 51 on a cyclical industrial is richly priced; mean-reversion risk is high after a 170% run.
- vol30d of 49.8% is very elevated — moves of this size in a megacap signal crowded, fragile positioning.
- Net debt/equity 209% — leveraged balance sheet if the cycle turns.
- FCF yield only 1.8%; you're paying up heavily for the growth.

**Insider / news read** — No insider signal (US name). Only material news is the 8% dividend hike — incrementally positive but not a thesis driver. Nothing fundamental explains a +48% three-month surge.

**Price read** — Parabolic and 2.3% off its 52w high, +50% above 200MA. Buying here is buying the very top of an extended move. The risk/reward into the +38-day earnings print is poor: any disappointment against a 51x multiple is brutal.

**Verdict** — Great company, terrible entry; momentum is real but fully discounted and the multiple is extreme. WATCH — not actionable now.

```json
}
```

--- Analyst note on AMAT (Applied Materials) ---
# Applied Materials (AMAT) — Research Note

**Thesis.** AMAT is the picks-and-shovels play on the AI capex cycle, and momentum has gone vertical — +54.5% in a month, +106% in three, sitting exactly at its 52w high. The new AI-chip systems launch plus the EssilorLuxottica AR partnership are giving the tape fresh narrative fuel. But the move has massively outrun the fundamentals (3.3% revenue growth, P/E ~65), so this is a momentum/sentiment bet, not a value one.

**Positives**
- Genuine secular tailwind: semicap equipment is structurally levered to the AI buildout.
- Pristine balance sheet — net cash, 30% operating margin, 35.6% ROE.
- Fresh catalysts landing (AI chip systems, AR glasses partnership) keeping flows hot.
- Tape is the strongest signal here: at 52w high on heavy turnover ($5.7B/mo).

**Concerns**
- Valuation is stretched to absurd: P/E ~65 against +3.3% YoY revenue and +3.2% 3y CAGR. The earnings haven't moved; the multiple has.
- 30d vol of 85% — this is a parabolic move that can reverse 20%+ in days.
- Entering at +0.0% from 52w high after a 3m double = textbook chase risk. Per §6, never chase a missed move.
- No insider data (US name) — can't lean on that edge.
- Next earnings ~99 days out; no near-term fundamental catalyst to validate the price.

**Insider / news read.** No insider signal — US name. News is a steady drumbeat of M3 product/partnership items (AI chip systems, AR glasses) plus pure price-action headlines ("surges 10%"). Supportive of narrative, but several are just describing the move, not driving new fundamentals.

**Price read.** Parabolic: +278% over 12m, +106% over 3m, +109% above the 200-day MA, pinned at the 52w high. This is extremely extended — the easy money was made well below here. Buying at the top of a 2x quarter is exactly the chase the constitution warns against.

**Verdict.** Strong company, real AI tailwind, but the price has tripled in three months on a stagnant earnings base — I have no edge entering at the absolute high. WATCH — not actionable now.

--- Analyst note on MU (Micron) ---
# Micron (MU) — Research Note

**Thesis.** Micron is the purest large-cap memory play on the AI buildout, and the R12 numbers are staggering: revenue +167% YoY, 65.7% operating margin, 55.9% net margin — these are unheard-of for a historically cyclical commodity memory maker. The market is pricing an extended HBM/DRAM super-cycle into 2H26. At P/E 25.5 on peak-ish earnings the stock is *not* obviously expensive on current numbers — but it has gone +809% in 12 months, which is the whole problem.

**Positives**
- Memory margins (72.6% gross) are at cycle-extreme highs — AI/HBM demand is real and structural, not a one-quarter blip.
- Net cash balance sheet (-19.6B net debt) — no financial fragility into any downturn.
- Just beat clearly and surged; momentum, fundamentals and news flow all aligned right now.
- P/E 25.5 is undemanding *if* this earnings level holds.

**Concerns**
- Memory is cyclical — 55.9% net margins are a cycle peak, not a baseline. P/E 25.5 on peak EPS is genuinely expensive on normalized earnings.
- +809%/12m and +220%/3m means I am being asked to chase a stock that has already run enormously; entering at -5.6% off 52w high is buying near the top.
- vol30d 123.7% — anything can happen here; gap risk both ways is huge.
- Next earnings ~191 days away — no near-term fundamental catalyst to re-rate; the move is largely behind us.

**Insider / news read.** No insider signal — US name. News is uniformly bullish (clear beat, market cap past Meta, "third most profitable US company"), all M3. This is consensus euphoria, which cuts both ways — it confirms the move but means the easy money is made.

**Price read.** 1145, +24% in a month, near 52w high, 166% above the 200ma. This is a vertical, late-stage move on a cyclical name. Buying here is chasing — exactly what §6 warns against.

**Verdict.** Real business, real cycle, but the move is largely spent and entering on a +24% monthly spike near the high is chasing — WATCH — not actionable now.

```json
  "key_concern": "Up 809% in 12m and 24% in the last month near 52w high — buying here chases a vertical, late-cycle move on peak earnings.",
```

--- Analyst note on PANW (Palo Alto Networks) ---
# Palo Alto Networks (PANW) — Analyst Note

**Thesis** — Genuine AI-security demand inflection: management raised FY guidance on 2026-06-03 citing strong AI-related demand, and the stock has responded with a violent +125.8% 3-month run to a fresh 52w high. The fundamental story is real (top-tier cybersecurity name, accelerating revenue), but the price has already discounted an enormous amount — this is a momentum chase into a parabolic move, not a fresh entry.

**Positives**
- FY guidance raised on AI demand — a hard, recent catalyst, not vibes.
- Revenue 19.5% YoY with 71.9% gross margin; net-cash balance sheet (-$1.04B net debt).
- Strong FCF ($3.1B R12) supports the quality of the franchise.
- New Deutsche Telekom AI-security platform expands European TAM.

**Concerns**
- P/E of 279 and FCF yield of 1.3% — valuation is extreme; almost nothing left for error.
- 3m +125.8%, +66.6% above 200MA, vol 56% — this is a parabolic, late-stage move that mean-reverts hard.
- Next earnings ~191 days out — no near-term fundamental catalyst to refresh the move; the guidance raise is already 4 weeks old and priced.
- No US insider signal to corroborate.

**Insider / news read** — No insider data (US name). News is supportive: guidance raise (M4) and a DT AI partnership (M3), both within 30 days — these explain the move but are already in the tape.

**Price read** — Exactly at 52w high, +28.8% in one month alone, +66% over 200MA. Buying here means paying the top tick of a vertical run with no upcoming catalyst for ~6 months. Risk/reward at $332 is poor; a pullback toward the $260–280 zone would be a far better entry on the same thesis.

**Verdict** — The thesis is real but the price is extended and there's no near-term catalyst; not a place to add conviction. WATCH — not actionable now.

response_text:

**Decisions:**
- **Sell VOLV-B (15 sh @ 324.60)** — Scout-flagged weak holder, flat 1m, smallest meaningful conviction; Industrials already crowded (HEXA+LIFCO). Free capital for fresher insider clusters.
- **Hold HEXA** — Scout says rotate, but it's a 2-day entry on a real 4-buyer cluster (CFO incl.) explicitly thesized as a *washout below 200MA*; Jul 25 print resolves it. Selling on the same setup I bought 2 days ago is churn, not thesis-break. Keep.
- **Buy KJELL (350 sh @ 12.25)** — best-in-class insider cluster (CEO×2, CFO, largest shareholder, zero sellers) into Jul 24 catalyst. Starter, respecting thin liquidity + 50% vol.
- **Buy NOLA-B (90 sh @ 48.45)** — Herenco controlling-family ~25M SEK into -24% drawdown; starter on signal, Aug 5 catalyst.
- **Add SCST (15 sh @ 145.40)** — controlling owner + 9 insiders, intensifying; top up the starter ahead of ~Jul 28 print. Staples now ~13% (ESSITY+SCST), well under 40% cap.
- **Hold STORY-B** — already 15%, near single-name conviction sizing; buyout reference caps add-appetite. No add.

Deploys ~22k, cash to ~15%, keeps the earnings-cluster book fully positioned.
